category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Dorsal Hand Cellulitis — Responding to Antibiotics
guideline_bullet: Uncomplicated cellulitis or abscess without concern for deep space infection or flexor tenosynovitis
clinical_presentation: 60-year-old female presents with 2 days of redness and warmth spreading over the dorsum of the left hand following a minor abrasion sustained while gardening. On examination, there is a 6 x 4 cm area of erythema with mild surrounding edema. The skin is intact without fluctuance or crepitus. She has full active and passive range of motion of all digits without pain. There is no tenderness along any flexor tendon sheath and all Kanavel signs are negative (no fusiform swelling, no flexed posture, no sheath tenderness, no pain with passive extension). Temperature is 98.6°F. WBC is 9,200 with a normal differential. She was started on oral cephalexin by her PCP yesterday and the erythema has not progressed.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncomplicated cellulitis or abscess without concern for deep space infection or flexor tenosynovitis

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Superficial Dorsal Hand Abscess — Drained in ED
guideline_bullet: Uncomplicated cellulitis or abscess without concern for deep space infection or flexor tenosynovitis
clinical_presentation: 34-year-old male presents with painful, fluctuant swelling on the dorsum of the right hand that developed over 3 days following a small puncture wound. On examination, there is a 2 cm subcutaneous abscess with surrounding erythema limited to the dorsal hand. The abscess is superficial and does not track to the deep spaces. He has full active range of motion of all digits without pain. Passive extension of each finger is painless. There is no tenderness along the flexor tendon sheaths. Temperature is 99.1°F. Under local anesthesia, the abscess is incised, 3 mL of purulent material is drained, and the cavity is irrigated and packed.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncomplicated cellulitis or abscess without concern for deep space infection or flexor tenosynovitis

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Web Space Cellulitis — Superficial, No Deep Space Concern
guideline_bullet: Uncomplicated cellulitis or abscess without concern for deep space infection or flexor tenosynovitis
clinical_presentation: 45-year-old female daycare worker presents with erythema and tenderness in the first web space of the right hand after a minor laceration 4 days ago. On examination, the first web space is mildly erythematous and tender. There is no fluctuance, no dorsal swelling to suggest collar-button abscess formation, and no loss of web space concavity. She can fully abduct and adduct the thumb and index finger without pain. All digits have full range of motion. No Kanavel signs are present. She is afebrile with a WBC of 8,400.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncomplicated cellulitis or abscess without concern for deep space infection or flexor tenosynovitis

title: Cellulitis Surrounding a Healing Laceration
guideline_bullet: Uncomplicated cellulitis or abscess without concern for deep space infection or flexor tenosynovitis
clinical_presentation: 52-year-old male returns 3 days after receiving sutures for a 3 cm dorsal hand laceration. He has developed erythema extending 1 cm around the wound edges. On examination, there is mild peri-wound erythema and warmth without fluctuance or purulent drainage. The sutures are intact. He has full active range of motion of all digits without pain. Neurovascular examination is intact. No Kanavel signs. Temperature is 98.8°F. WBC is 10,100.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncomplicated cellulitis or abscess without concern for deep space infection or flexor tenosynovitis

title: Acute Paronychia — Index Finger
guideline_bullet: Paronychia
clinical_presentation: 55-year-old female presents with 3 days of increasing pain, redness, and swelling along the lateral nail fold of the right index finger. She reports she had a hangnail that she pulled several days ago. On examination, there is an erythematous, fluctuant collection along the eponychial fold with expressible purulent material. The finger is otherwise non-tender along the flexor tendon sheath. She has full active range of motion of the finger, and passive extension of the digit is painless. She is afebrile.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Paronychia

title: Paronychia — Thumb, Nail Biter
guideline_bullet: Paronychia
clinical_presentation: 22-year-old male college student presents with a painful, swollen radial nail fold of the right thumb. He has a history of chronic nail biting. On examination, there is a small collection of purulence visible beneath the cuticle of the thumb. The nail plate is intact. The thumb is otherwise non-tender along the flexor pollicis longus tendon sheath. Range of motion of the thumb IP and MCP joints is full. He is afebrile.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Paronychia

guideline_bullet: Paronychia
clinical_presentation: 38-year-old female presents with 2 days of throbbing pain at the nail fold of the left ring finger. She recalls aggressive trimming of the cuticle during a recent manicure. On examination, there is a tense, erythematous collection at the radial eponychial fold with 1 cm of surrounding erythema on the dorsal proximal phalanx. There is no tenderness along the flexor tendon sheath. Passive extension is painless. Active range of motion is full. She is afebrile.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Paronychia

title: Paronychia — Small Finger, Diabetic Patient
guideline_bullet: Paronychia
clinical_presentation: 65-year-old male with well-controlled type 2 diabetes (HbA1c 6.0%) presents with a 2-day history of pain and swelling at the eponychial fold of the right small finger. On examination, there is a small fluctuant collection beneath the cuticle. The finger has full range of motion without pain on passive extension. There is no flexor tendon sheath tenderness. Temperature is 98.4°F. Blood glucose is 142 mg/dL.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Paronychia

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Dorsal Hand Laceration — No Deep Structure Injury
guideline_bullet: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury
clinical_presentation: 35-year-old male presents after sustaining a 4 cm laceration to the dorsum of the right hand while slicing vegetables. The wound is clean with well-perfused edges. On examination, he has full active range of motion of all digits against resistance, including finger extension and flexion at all joints. Two-point discrimination is intact in all digital nerve distributions. No tendon is visible in the wound base, which appears to involve subcutaneous tissue only. The hand and fingers are warm and well perfused, and capillary refill is 2 seconds in all digits. X-ray shows no fracture or foreign body.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Volar Finger Laceration — Intact Tendons and Nerves
guideline_bullet: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury
clinical_presentation: 28-year-old female sous chef presents after a knife slipped and lacerated the volar surface of her left middle finger at the level of the middle phalanx. On examination, there is a 1.5 cm transverse laceration. She has full active flexion at the DIP joint (FDP intact) and isolated PIP flexion with the other fingers held in extension (FDS intact). Extension is full. Two-point discrimination is 4 mm on both the radial and ulnar sides of the lacerated digit (normal) proximal and distal to the laceration, and 4 mm in the uninjured digits. Capillary refill is 2 seconds in all digits including the injured digit. X-ray does not demonstrate fracture or foreign body
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation

title: Thenar Eminence Laceration — Superficial
guideline_bullet: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury
clinical_presentation: 41-year-old male presents after cutting the thenar eminence of his right hand on a broken ceramic plate. On examination, there is a 3 cm laceration over the thenar eminence that extends through skin and subcutaneous fat only. The laceration is hemostatic. The thenar muscles are not violated. Thumb is well perfused with normal capillary refill. He has full opposition, abduction, and flexion of the thumb against resistance. Sensation is intact in the median and radial nerve distributions. X-ray shows no fracture or foreign body.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation

title: Multiple Superficial Lacerations — Glass Injury
guideline_bullet: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury
clinical_presentation: 19-year-old female presents after falling onto broken glass. She has three superficial lacerations on the dorsum of the left hand ranging from 1 to 3 cm in length. On examination, all wounds are confined to skin and subcutaneous tissue. She has full active range of motion of all digits against resistance. Sensation is intact and symmetric throughout. All digits are well-perfused. X-ray shows no retained glass fragments and no fractures.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation

title: PIP Joint Sprain — Basketball Injury
guideline_bullet: Sprains, contusions, or mild hand edema
clinical_presentation: 19-year-old male basketball player presents after jamming his right ring finger while catching a pass. He has swelling and tenderness over the PIP joint. Active range of motion is mildly limited by pain but full flexion and extension can be achieved. The joint is stable to radial and ulnar stress testing in full extension and 30 degrees of flexion. There is no volar plate tenderness suggestive of instability. Three-view finger x-rays show no fracture or dislocation. Capillary refill and sensation are intact.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Sprains, contusions, or mild hand edema

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Dorsal Hand Contusion — Punch Injury, Negative X-Ray
guideline_bullet: Sprains, contusions, or mild hand edema
clinical_presentation: 28-year-old female presents after punching a wall during an argument. She reports pain and swelling over the dorsum of the left hand. On examination, there is mild dorsal hand edema and tenderness to palpation over the 4th metacarpal head and neck. She has full active range of motion of all digits without significant pain. There is no rotational deformity when she makes a composite fist. The knuckle contour is preserved. Three-view hand x-rays are negative for fracture or dislocation.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Sprains, contusions, or mild hand edema

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Thumb MCP Sprain — Stable to Stress Testing
guideline_bullet: Sprains, contusions, or mild hand edema
clinical_presentation: 32-year-old male skier presents after a fall in which his left thumb was forcefully abducted by the ski pole strap. He has pain and mild swelling at the thumb MCP joint. On examination, he has tenderness over the ulnar aspect of the thumb MCPJ. . Stress testing of the thumb ulnar collateral ligament (UCL) in extension and in 30 degrees of flexion reveals a firm endpoint with less than 15 degrees of laxity compared to the contralateral side. There is no Stener lesion palpable. X-rays show no fracture or avulsion fragment. Active range of motion of the thumb is normal, although painful.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Sprains, contusions, or mild hand edema

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Mild Hand Edema After Blunt Trauma
guideline_bullet: Sprains, contusions, or mild hand edema
clinical_presentation: 48-year-old male construction worker presents after his right hand was struck by a falling 2x4 plank. He has diffuse mild swelling of the dorsal hand with moderate ecchymosis. On examination, there is no point tenderness over any metacarpal. He can make a full composite fist and extend all digits fully against resistance. Sensation and perfusion are intact throughout. Three-view hand and wrist x-rays are negative for fracture or dislocation.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Sprains, contusions, or mild hand edema

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Wood Splinter Removed from Palm
guideline_bullet: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement
clinical_presentation: 42-year-old male carpenter presents with a wooden splinter embedded in the thenar eminence of his right hand sustained 2 hours ago. The splinter is partially visible at the skin surface. Under local anesthesia, the entire splinter is removed in one piece using fine forceps. Post-removal examination shows full range of motion of the thumb and all fingers. Sensation is intact. X-ray (obtained to rule out additional fragments) shows no residual foreign body. The wound is irrigated and dressed.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Glass Fragment Removed from Dorsal Finger
guideline_bullet: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement
clinical_presentation: 25-year-old female presents with a small glass fragment embedded in the subcutaneous tissue of the dorsal aspect of the right index finger overlying the proximal phalanx, sustained when she dropped a drinking glass. The fragment is visible on x-ray just deep to the skin. Under digital block, a small incision is made and the 4 mm glass fragment is removed intact. Post-removal x-ray confirms no residual fragments or foreign bodies. She has full active range of motion, intact sensation, and normal perfusion before and after fragment removal.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Metal Shaving Removed from Hypothenar Eminence
guideline_bullet: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement
clinical_presentation: 37-year-old male machinist presents with a metallic foreign body in the hypothenar eminence sustained while operating a lathe. X-ray shows a 3 mm superficial metallic fragment. Under local anesthesia with fluoroscopic guidance, the fragment is located and removed in its entirety. Before and after removal, he has full active range of motion of all digits including small finger abduction (ulnar nerve motor function intact). Sensation in the ulnar nerve distribution is normal. The wound is irrigated and closed.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement

category_label: DOES NOT Necessitate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: Does not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
title: Fish Hook Removed from Index Fingertip
guideline_bullet: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement
clinical_presentation: 55-year-old male recreational fisherman presents with a fish hook embedded in the pulp of the left index fingertip. The barb has not penetrated through to the dorsal aspect of the digit. Using the advance-and-cut technique under digital block, the hook is removed completely. Before and after removal, he has intact sensation throughout the fingertip and normal capillary refill. The FDP tendon is intact (full DIP flexion against resistance). X-ray confirms no retained metallic fragments.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement

title: FDP Laceration — Single Digit, Ring Finger
guideline_bullet: Single digit injury with tendon and/or digital nerve involvement
clinical_presentation: 30-year-old male presents after a broken drinking glass lacerated the volar surface of his right ring finger at the level of the proximal phalanx. On examination, there is a 2 cm transverse laceration with bleeding controlled by direct pressure. He is unable to flex the DIP joint of the ring finger against resistance, and the FDP tendon is not palpable with attempted flexion. FDS function is intact (isolated PIP flexion present). Two-point discrimination is intact at 5 mm on both radial and ulnar sides of the digit including distal to the laceration. Capillary refill is brisk. X-ray shows no fracture or foreign body.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Single digit injury with tendon and/or digital nerve involvement

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Isolated Ulnar Digital Nerve Laceration — Single Digit
guideline_bullet: Single digit injury with tendon and/or digital nerve involvement
clinical_presentation: 40-year-old female presents after a kitchen knife lacerated the ulnar border of her right ring finger at the level of the proximal phalanx. On examination, there is a 1.5 cm laceration on the ulnar side. She has full active flexion and extension against resistance — both FDP and FDS are intact. However, two-point discrimination on the ulnar side of the ring finger is greater than 15 mm (contralateral side is 4 mm), consistent with an ulnar digital nerve injury. The radial digital nerve is intact. The digit is well-perfused and there is no active bleeding. X-ray is negative for fracture or dislocation.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Single digit injury with tendon and/or digital nerve involvement

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Partial Extensor Tendon Laceration — Single Digit, Middle Finger
guideline_bullet: Single digit injury with tendon and/or digital nerve involvement
clinical_presentation: 48-year-old male presents with a 2 cm transverse laceration over the dorsum of the right hand at the level of the middle finger MCP joint from sheet metal at work. On careful examination with the finger flexed, a partial laceration (approximately 50%) of the extensor digitorum communis tendon to the middle finger is identified. He can extend the middle finger at the MCP joint but with weakness compared to the contralateral side and a 15-degree extensor lag. No other digits are affected. Sensation and perfusion are intact. X-ray is negative for fracture or dislocation.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Single digit injury with tendon and/or digital nerve involvement

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: 5th Metacarpal Neck Fracture (Boxer's Fracture)
guideline_bullet: Closed carpal, metacarpal, or phalangeal fractures
clinical_presentation: 22-year-old male presents after punching a concrete wall. He has pain and swelling over the ulnar aspect of the right hand. On examination, there is tenderness and swelling over the 5th metacarpal head with loss of knuckle prominence. The overlying skin is intact without laceration (specifically, there is no fight-bite wound over the MCP joint). He can make a fist without rotational deformity or scissoring of the digits. X-rays show a 5th metacarpal neck fracture with 30 degrees of angulation. Sensation and perfusion are intact. An ulnar gutter splint is applied.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Closed carpal, metacarpal, or phalangeal fractures

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Scaphoid Fracture — Closed
guideline_bullet: Closed carpal, metacarpal, or phalangeal fractures
clinical_presentation: 27-year-old male presents after falling off his bicycle onto an outstretched right hand. He has pain in the radial wrist. On examination, there is tenderness in the anatomic snuffbox and with axial loading of the thumb. The skin is intact. Wrist range of motion is limited by pain. Finger function is intact. Neurovascular examination is normal. Initial x-rays show a non-displaced waist fracture of the scaphoid. A thumb spica splint is applied.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Closed carpal, metacarpal, or phalangeal fractures

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Proximal Phalanx Fracture — Closed, Ring Finger
guideline_bullet: Closed carpal, metacarpal, or phalangeal fractures
clinical_presentation: 35-year-old female fell while rock climbing and sustained a twisting injury to her left ring finger. On examination, the proximal phalanx is tender to palpation and edematous. The skin is intact. X-rays show a spiral fracture of the proximal phalanx of the ring finger. There is 10 degrees of rotational malalignment visible on clinical exam. Sensation and perfusion are intact. The finger is buddy-taped and placed in an intrinsic-plus splint.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Closed carpal, metacarpal, or phalangeal fractures

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Non-Displaced Distal Radius Fracture
guideline_bullet: Closed non-displaced distal radius or ulnar fractures; displaced fractures that have been reduced without soft tissue compromise
clinical_presentation: 45-year-old female presents after a fall on an outstretched hand. She has wrist pain and swelling. On examination, there is tenderness over the distal radius without gross deformity. The skin is intact without tenting or compromise. Sensation is intact in the median, ulnar, and radial nerve distributions. There is no evidence of acute carpal tunnel syndrome. Capillary refill is normal in all digits. X-rays show a non-displaced extra-articular distal radius fracture. A well-padded sugar-tong splint is applied.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Closed non-displaced distal radius or ulnar fractures; displaced fractures that have been reduced without soft tissue compromise

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Displaced Distal Radius Fracture — Successfully Reduced
guideline_bullet: Closed non-displaced distal radius or ulnar fractures; displaced fractures that have been reduced without soft tissue compromise
clinical_presentation: 62-year-old female presents after slipping on ice. She has a dinner-fork deformity of the left wrist. X-rays show a dorsally displaced extra-articular distal radius fracture with 25 degrees of dorsal angulation and 8 mm of shortening. Neurovascular exam is intact with no evidence of acute carpal tunnel syndrome. Under hematoma block, closed reduction is performed. Post-reduction x-rays show restoration of radial length and inclination with acceptable alignment. The skin is intact, well-perfused, and without tenting. Finger function is normal. A sugar-tong splint is applied. Post-reduction neurovascular exam remains normal without carpal tunnel symptoms
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Closed non-displaced distal radius or ulnar fractures; displaced fractures that have been reduced without soft tissue compromise

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Mallet Finger — Tendinous, Long Finger
guideline_bullet: Mallet finger injuries
clinical_presentation: 50-year-old female presents after her left long finger was forcibly flexed while tucking in a bedsheet. She noticed an immediate inability to straighten the fingertip. On examination, the long finger distal interphalangeal (DIP) joint rests in approximately 30 degrees of flexion. She cannot actively extend the DIP joint, but passive extension is full and painless. She is able to actively flex the DIP and proximal interphalangeal joints. There is mild dorsal swelling at the DIP joint. X-rays show no fracture; the joint is congruent. She is placed in a digital extension splint of the DIP joint.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Mallet finger injuries

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Mallet Finger — Bony, Small Finger
guideline_bullet: Mallet finger injuries
clinical_presentation: 29-year-old male softball player presents after the ball struck the tip of his right small finger while fielding. The DIP joint rests in flexion and he cannot actively extend it. Active flexion is intact. X-rays reveal a dorsal avulsion fracture involving approximately 25% of the articular surface of the distal phalanx. The joint is congruent on lateral view (no volar subluxation of the distal phalanx). Neurovascular examination is intact. He is placed in a digital extension splint of the DIP joint.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Mallet finger injuries

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Open Tuft Fracture — Crushed Fingertip, Index Finger
guideline_bullet: Open tuft (distal phalanx) fractures
clinical_presentation: 8-year-old female presents after her right index fingertip was crushed in a car door. On examination, there is a subungual hematoma involving greater than 50% of the nail, with a visible nailbed laceration beneath. The nail is partially avulsed. X-ray reveals a comminuted distal phalanx tuft fracture. The fracture is open through the nailbed laceration. The digit is well-perfused with intact capillary refill. Sensation is intact distally. She can flex and extend the DIP joint (fracture does not extend to the articular surface). Under digital block, the nail is removed, the nailbed is repaired with absorbable suture, and the nail plate is replaced as a splint. Dressings are applied and the distal interphalangeal joint is splinted with an aluminum splint with the proximal interphalangeal joint free.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Open tuft (distal phalanx) fractures

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Open Tuft Fracture — Middle Fingertip Crush
guideline_bullet: Open tuft (distal phalanx) fractures
clinical_presentation: 12-year-old male presents after slamming his right middle fingertip in a heavy door at school. On examination, there is a crush injury to the fingertip with a stellate laceration at the tip and a subungual hematoma. X-ray reveals a comminuted tuft fracture of the distal phalanx with the fracture open through the laceration. The digit is well-perfused. Sensation is grossly intact. The DIP joint is congruent. The nail plate is removed, the laceration is irrigated and closed with absorbable suture. The nail is replaced as a stent and a fingertip splint is placed with the proximal interphalangeal joint free.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Open tuft (distal phalanx) fractures

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Reduced Dorsal PIP Dislocation — Middle Finger
guideline_bullet: CMC/MP/PIP joint dislocations that have been reduced
clinical_presentation: 38-year-old male recreational football player presents after a hyperextension injury to his right middle finger. Pre-reduction x-rays showed a dorsal dislocation of the PIP joint without fracture. Under digital block, the joint was reduced without complication. Post-reduction x-rays confirm a congruent joint. On post-reduction exam, the joint is stable to gentle stress testing in extension and 30 degrees of flexion. Active range of motion is limited by pain but the joint does not subluxate. The PIP joint is splinted in 30 degrees of flexion, and this appears stable.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: CMC/MP/PIP joint dislocations that have been reduced

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Reduced MCP Dislocation — Index Finger
guideline_bullet: CMC/MP/PIP joint dislocations that have been reduced
clinical_presentation: 25-year-old female gymnast presents after a hyperextension injury to the left index finger during a vault. X-rays show a dorsal dislocation of the index MCP joint. The dislocation appears simple (not complex) on x-ray — the sesamoids are not interposed in the joint. Under sedation, closed reduction is achieved with wrist flexion and direct pressure on the proximal phalanx base. Post-reduction x-rays show a congruent joint. The MCP joint is stable and the patient can actively flex the finger. Neurovascular exam is intact. The finger is splinted in a dorsal extension blocking splint at 60 degrees of flexion.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: CMC/MP/PIP joint dislocations that have been reduced

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up

title: Retained Needle in Thenar Eminence
guideline_bullet: Foreign bodies unable to be easily removed
clinical_presentation: 27-year-old female presents after a sewing needle broke off in the thenar eminence of her right hand. X-ray reveals a 1.5 cm metallic foreign body embedded deep within the thenar musculature. Under local anesthesia, exploration is attempted but the foreign body cannot be located despite careful dissection under fluoroscopic guidance. The wound is thoroughly irrigated and closed. She has intact sensation and motor function of the thumb and fingers. There are no signs of infection.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Foreign bodies unable to be easily removed

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Retained Glass Fragment — Deep in Palm
guideline_bullet: Foreign bodies unable to be easily removed
clinical_presentation: 31-year-old male presents after sustaining a laceratin to his palm on broken glass 6 hours ago. The superficial laceration was closed at an urgent care, but he now has persistent sharp pain with gripping. X-ray shows a 6 mm glass fragment deep within the midpalmar space, adjacent to the flexor tendons. Attempted removal in the ED under local anesthesia is unsuccessful due to depth and concern for possible proximity to neurovascular structures. Sensation and motor function are intact. The fingers are well perfused.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Foreign bodies unable to be easily removed

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Index Finger Felon — Drained
guideline_bullet: Felon (if decompressed)
clinical_presentation: 44-year-old male presents with 4 days of progressive pain and swelling of the right index fingertip. On examination, the fingertip pulp is tense, erythematous, and exquisitely tender. There is no fluctuance along the flexor tendon sheath and no Kanavel signs. Under digital block, a unilateral longitudinal incision is made and a pocket of purulent material is drained and cultures are obtained. The septae of the pulp space are disrupted. The wound is irrigated and packed with iodoform gauze. Post-procedure, the tenseness has resolved and pain is significantly improved. The patient is discharged with 7 days of oral antibiotics and directions to perform warm water soaks.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Felon (if decompressed)

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Thumb Felon — Drained
guideline_bullet: Felon (if decompressed)
clinical_presentation: 58-year-old female with a history of a thorn prick to the right thumb pulp 5 days ago presents with a tense, erythematous, fluctuant thumb tip. She is afebrile. No Kanavel signs are present. Under digital block, incision and drainage yields 2 mL of purulent material and cultures are obtained. The pulp decompresses well and pain improves immediately. The wound is irrigated and loosely packed.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Felon (if decompressed)

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Nailbed Laceration — Thumb, Hammer Injury
guideline_bullet: Nail and nailbed injuries
clinical_presentation: 33-year-old male struck his left thumb with a hammer while doing home repairs. He presents with a large subungual hematoma involving approximately 60% of the nail plate, with the nail partially detached from the nailbed. There is an underlying nailbed laceration visible. X-ray of the thumb shows no fracture. The digit is well-perfused with intact sensation. Under digital block, the nail is removed and a stellate nailbed laceration is repaired with 6-0 chromic suture. The nail plate is replaced and secured with a suture through the hyponychium.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Nail and nailbed injuries

category_label: MAY NOT Necessitate Immediate Consult but Requires Follow-Up
category_description: May not necessitate hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter but most often requires hand surgery follow-up.
title: Nail Avulsion with Germinal Matrix Injury — Ring Finger
guideline_bullet: Nail and nailbed injuries
clinical_presentation: 16-year-old female soccer player presents after her right ring fingernail was caught and avulsed while diving for a ball. The nail has been completely pulled off, and the germinal matrix is lacerated with a transverse laceration at the base of the nail fold. X-ray shows no fracture. The nailbed is otherwise intact distally. The wound is irrigated, the germinal matrix is approximated with absorbable suture, and a non-adherent dressing with foil is placed as a splint in the nail fold to prevent adhesion.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Nail and nailbed injuries

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Deep Space Infection — Thenar and Midpalmar Space
guideline_bullet: Suspected deep space or necrotizing infections
clinical_presentation: 52-year-old female with poorly controlled diabetes presents with 4 days of worsening right hand pain and swelling following a small puncture wound to the palm. On examination, the thenar and midpalmar spaces are markedly swollen, tense, and tender. The hand is held in a position of rest with the fingers slightly flexed. There is erythema and edema extending to the dorsum of the hand. She is febrile to 101.8°F with chills. WBC is 16,800 with a left shift. Blood glucose is 340 mg/dL. The swelling and tenderness are not isolated to a single flexor tendon sheath but involve the deep palmar spaces diffusely.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Suspected deep space or necrotizing infections

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Necrotizing Fasciitis — Dorsal Hand
guideline_bullet: Suspected deep space or necrotizing infections
clinical_presentation: 63-year-old male with poorly controlled diabetes and peripheral vascular disease presents with 2 days of rapidly spreading erythema, pain out of proportion to exam findings, and dusky discoloration of the dorsal right hand after a minor laceration. On examination, there are hemorrhagic bullae over the dorsum of the hand. The skin has areas of violaceous and dark discoloration. Crepitus is palpable on exam. He is febrile to 103.2°F, tachycardic at 115, and hypotensive at 88/52 mmHg. WBC is 22,400. Lactate is 4.1 mmol/L. X-ray shows subcutaneous gas tracking along the fascial planes extending into the forearm.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Suspected deep space or necrotizing infections

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Flexor Tenosynovitis — Index Finger, Kanavel Signs
guideline_bullet: Suspected flexor tenosynovitis
clinical_presentation: 68-year-old male with diabetes presents with 3 days of progressive swelling and pain in the right index finger after a puncture wound from a rose thorn. On examination, the index finger demonstrates four Kanavel signs: fusiform (sausage-shaped) swelling of the entire finger, the finger is held in a flexed posture, there is tenderness along the entire flexor tendon sheath from the A1 pulley to the DIP crease, and passive extension of the finger elicits severe pain. He is febrile to 101.2°F. WBC is 14,500 with a left shift. CRP is 8.2 mg/dL.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Suspected flexor tenosynovitis

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Septic Wrist Joint
guideline_bullet: Suspected septic wrist, CMC, MP, PIP, or DIP joints
clinical_presentation: 29-year-old male with a history of IV drug use presents with 2 days of progressive right wrist pain, swelling, and inability to move the wrist. He is febrile to 102.5°F. On examination, the wrist is diffusely swollen, erythematous, and warm. Range of motion is severely limited and painful in all planes. There is significant pain with axial loading of the wrist. There is no isolated flexor tendon sheath tenderness; the entire wrist joint appears involved. WBC is 18,200 with 88% neutrophils. CRP is 15.4 mg/dL. Wrist x-ray shows soft tissue swelling and a joint effusion without osteomyelitis.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Suspected septic wrist, CMC, MP, PIP, or DIP joints

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Septic PIP Joint — Middle Finger
guideline_bullet: Suspected septic wrist, CMC, MP, PIP, or DIP joints
clinical_presentation: 45-year-old female presents with 3 days of progressive pain, swelling, and redness at the PIP joint of the left middle finger following a cat bite directly over the joint. On examination, the PIP joint is erythematous, warm, and markedly swollen. She holds the finger in slight flexion. Active and passive range of motion of the PIP joint are severely limited and exquisitely painful. There is significant pain with axial loading of the PIP joint.The swelling is localized to the joint rather than the entire tendon sheath . She is febrile to 100.8°F. WBC is 13,200.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Suspected septic wrist, CMC, MP, PIP, or DIP joints

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Multi-Digit Extensor Tendon Lacerations
guideline_bullet: Injury to multiple digits with tendon and/or nerve involvement
clinical_presentation: 36-year-old male presents after a glass panel fell and lacerated the dorsum of his right hand transversely at the level of the MCP joints. On examination, there is a 12 cm dorsal laceration with exposed extensor tendons. He is unable to extend the index, middle, or ring fingers at the MCP joints. Small finger extension is intact. Sensation is intact in all digital nerve distributions. All digits are well-perfused. X-ray shows no fracture.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Injury to multiple digits with tendon and/or nerve involvement

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Wrist Laceration with Absent Radial Pulse
guideline_bullet: Injuries with vascular compromise
clinical_presentation: 22-year-old female presents after sustaining a deep laceration to the volar wrist from a broken window. On examination, there is a 5 cm transverse laceration over the volar wrist at the wrist crease with brisk arterial bleeding controlled by direct pressure. The radial pulse is absent distally with no dopplerable signal. Ulnar doppler signal is present. The thumb and index finger have sluggish capillary refill at 5 seconds and appear pale compared to the other digits. She has diminished sensation in the median nerve distribution. She can weakly flex the index and middle finger DIP joints.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Injuries with vascular compromise

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Multi-Digit Amputation — Table Saw
guideline_bullet: Amputations
clinical_presentation: 28-year-old male presents after a table saw injury to the right hand. He has complete amputations of the index finger at the level of the proximal phalanx and the middle finger at the level of the PIP joint. The amputated parts have been recovered, wrapped in saline-moistened gauze, placed in a plastic bag, and stored on ice. The residual digits are bleeding but controlled with a compressive dressing. The ring and small fingers are intact with normal perfusion and sensation. X-rays confirm the levels of amputation without additional fractures or foreign bodies
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Amputations

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Degloving Injury — Dorsal Hand
guideline_bullet: Injuries with extensive soft tissue trauma
clinical_presentation: 42-year-old male industrial worker presents after his right hand was caught in a roller machine. On examination, there is a circumferential degloving injury of the dorsal hand from the wrist to the MCP joints. The skin and subcutaneous tissue have been avulsed, exposing the extensor tendons and metacarpal shafts. The degloved skin is partially attached at the ulnar border. Despite the extensive soft tissue loss, the digital arteries appear patent with capillary refill present in all fingertips. X-rays show no fractures.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Injuries with extensive soft tissue trauma

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Open Metacarpal Fractures — Circular Saw
guideline_bullet: Open wrist, hand, or digital fractures and open joint injuries (other than distal phalanx tuft fractures)
clinical_presentation: 55-year-old female presents after a circular saw laceration to the dorsum of the right hand. On examination, there is a 10 cm transverse laceration over the dorsal hand at the level of the metacarpal shafts with exposed bone. The 3rd and 4th metacarpals are visibly fractured through the wound. Extensor tendons to the middle and ring fingers are lacerated. The hand is well-perfused distally. Sensation is diminished in the first dorsal web space.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Open wrist, hand, or digital fractures and open joint injuries (other than distal phalanx tuft fractures)

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Perilunate Dislocation
guideline_bullet: Closed carpal dislocations (i.e. peri-lunate dislocations)
clinical_presentation: 40-year-old male fell from a height of 10 feet onto his outstretched right hand. He presents with significant wrist swelling and pain. On examination, there is diffuse wrist tenderness and limited range of motion. Median nerve function is diminished with decreased sensation in the thumb, index, and middle fingers, and weak thumb opposition. PA x-ray shows disruption of the normal carpal arcs — Gilula's lines are disrupted — and the lunate appears triangular rather than quadrilateral. The lateral view shows the capitate dislocated dorsally relative to the lunate, which maintains its relationship with the radius.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Closed carpal dislocations (i.e. peri-lunate dislocations)

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Non-Reducible Displaced Distal Radius Fracture
guideline_bullet: Displaced, non-reducible distal radius fractures
clinical_presentation: 50-year-old male presents after a motorcycle accident with a grossly deformed left wrist. X-rays show a displaced, comminuted intra-articular distal radius fracture with 20 degrees of dorsal angulation, 10 mm of shortening, and a depressed lunate facet fragment. The overlying skin is tented. Two closed reduction attempts under conscious sedation have failed to restore acceptable alignment; post-reduction films continue to show significant articular step-off and shortening. Median nerve paresthesias are present and worsening.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Displaced, non-reducible distal radius fractures

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Closed Metacarpal Fracture with Skin Tenting
guideline_bullet: Closed fractures with soft tissue compromise
clinical_presentation: 34-year-old male presents after a crush injury to the right hand. X-rays show a significantly displaced 2nd metacarpal shaft fracture. On examination, the overlying skin is tented over the fracture fragment, appearing discolored with blanching at the apex. The skin has not been perforated but is under significant tension. Capillary refill in the index finger is mildly delayed at 3 seconds. Sensation is intact. The hand is moderately edematous
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Closed fractures with soft tissue compromise

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Irreducible Complex MCP Dislocation — Index Finger
guideline_bullet: Non-reducible or open radiocarpal, carpal, CMC, MP, or IP joint dislocations
clinical_presentation: 30-year-old male presents after a hyperextension injury to the left index finger. X-rays show a dorsal dislocation of the index MCP joint. On the PA view, the sesamoid bones appear widened and interposed within the joint space, suggesting a complex dislocation with volar plate interposition. Two closed reduction attempts under adequate sedation have failed. Post-attempt x-rays continue to show a dislocated joint. Neurovascular status of the finger is intact but the patient is in significant pain.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Non-reducible or open radiocarpal, carpal, CMC, MP, or IP joint dislocations

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Uncontrolled Forearm Bleeding — Chainsaw Laceration
guideline_bullet: Uncontrolled bleeding from an upper extremity
clinical_presentation: 41-year-old male presents after a chainsaw laceration to the right forearm. He has profuse arterial bleeding that has soaked through multiple dressings. A tourniquet was applied in the field by EMS. On examination with the tourniquet in place, there is a deep 12 cm laceration over the volar forearm with exposed muscle and multiple disrupted structures. Distal to the tourniquet, the hand is pale with absent radial and ulnar pulses. When the tourniquet is briefly loosened, brisk pulsatile bleeding resumes. Hemoglobin is 10.2 g/dL.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Uncontrolled bleeding from an upper extremity

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: High-Pressure Grease Gun Injection — Index Finger
guideline_bullet: High pressure injection injuries
clinical_presentation: 45-year-old male industrial worker presents 1 hour after accidentally injecting grease into the volar pad of his right index finger with a high-pressure grease gun operating at approximately 7,000 PSI. On initial examination, there is a small puncture wound on the volar fingertip with minimal external signs of injury. The finger is slightly swollen. He reports moderate pain. Range of motion is limited by discomfort. Sensation is intact. X-ray shows radiopaque material tracking along the flexor tendon sheath into the palm.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: High pressure injection injuries

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Hand Compartment Syndrome — Crush Injury
guideline_bullet: Suspected or confirmed compartment syndrome of the hand or forearm
clinical_presentation: 32-year-old male presents 6 hours after a crush injury to the left hand when it was caught in a hydraulic press. The hand is markedly swollen and tense. He has severe pain disproportionate to the apparent injury, dramatically worsened by passive extension of the fingers. The intrinsic muscles feel firm and tense on palpation. Two-point discrimination is diminished in the median and ulnar nerve distributions. Capillary refill is sluggish at 4 seconds. Intracompartment pressures measure 45 mmHg (diastolic BP is 70 mmHg, delta P = 25 mmHg). No surgeon with hand experience is available at the presenting facility.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Suspected or confirmed compartment syndrome of the hand or forearm

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Thumb Amputation with Ischemic Finger
guideline_bullet: Combined: Amputations + Injuries with vascular compromise
clinical_presentation: 38-year-old male woodworker presents after a band saw injury amputated his right thumb at the MCP joint level. The amputated part is recovered and appropriately stored. Additionally, the index finger has a deep volar laceration with absent digital pulses and doppler signals and a cool, pale appearance. Capillary refill in the index finger is absent. Sensation in the index finger is diminished. The remaining digits are intact.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Amputations; Injuries with vascular compromise

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Horseshoe Abscess — Connected Flexor Sheath Infection
guideline_bullet: Combined: Suspected flexor tenosynovitis + Suspected septic joint
clinical_presentation: 56-year-old male with diabetes presents with 5 days of progressive pain in both the right thumb and small finger. On examination, both the thumb and small finger demonstrate Kanavel signs: fusiform swelling, flexed posture, tendon sheath tenderness, and pain with passive extension. The space of Parona at the volar wrist is swollen and tender, suggesting communication between the radial and ulnar bursae. The wrist joint itself is not as involved. He is febrile to 102.0°F. WBC is 19,600. Glucose is 380 mg/dL.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Suspected flexor tenosynovitis; Suspected deep space or necrotizing infections

category_label: MAY Necessitate Immediate Hand Surgery Consultation
category_description: May necessitate immediate hand surgery consultation (or transfer if unavailable).
title: Mangled Hand Injury — Industrial Press
guideline_bullet: Combined: Injuries with extensive soft tissue trauma + Open fractures
clinical_presentation: 47-year-old male presents after his left hand was caught in a stamping press at a manufacturing plant. On examination, there is extensive soft tissue crush injury to the hand with multiple open fractures of the 2nd through 4th metacarpals. Extensor tendons are exposed and partially avulsed. There is a large area of devitalized skin and muscle on the dorsum. The small finger is amputated at the PIP level. The index and middle fingers have compromised perfusion with sluggish capillary refill. He is hemodynamically stable after fluid resuscitation. Xray confirms metacarpal fractures.
expected_guideline_bullets_pdf: Injuries with extensive soft tissue trauma; Open wrist, hand, or digital fractures and open joint injuries (other than distal phalanx tuft fractures)
